truly wild that one tenth of one billion people use Netflix without paying for it & the company didnt stress over it much for years until forced to they must be freaked about potential viewer flight to slow-roll their new approach https://www.nytimes.com/... https://www.nytimes.com/... https://twitter.com/...

Netflix comes across as strategically floundering these days. - Raising prices only for it to drive subscribers to quit the service. - Turning the product into Hulu with mass market ads. - Pivot to building franchises when their culture is cancel shows first, ask questions later https://twitter.com/...
If the melting ice cube that is $nflx can beat earnings, that should bode well for the rest of the market. https://twitter.com/...
$NFLX declined 77% in 6 months from last November As bad as that was, they declined 79% in 4 months in 2011 during the Qwikster debacle

Netflix didn't blow the roof off its second-quarter earnings. It announced it lost about 1 million global subscribers in Q2. But the stock jumped. That's what happens when expectations are so low $NFLX (via @sherman4949) https://www.cnbc.com/...

The ad play isn't just “we're missing subscribers.” Of course that's a point. But it's “we're missing subscribers, AND we're riding a plateau that dips into churn in our highest ARPU market pointing to a cap at about 75M compared to 100M once expected.” UCAN numbers are key.
“hey, what if you were to pay more money for the same or worse service with additional annoying restrictions?” “wait, where are you going?” https://www.bbc.com/...

“We're talking about losing 1 million instead of losing 2 million, so our excitement is tempered by the less-bad results,” Netflix Chairman and Co-Chief Executive Reed Hastings said in an analyst interview to discuss the results. https://www.wsj.com/... https://twitter.com/...

Netflix's 970K subscriber loss is even more negative when broken down by region. US/Canada: down 1.3 million EMEA: down 770,000 LATAM: up 10,000 APAC: up 1.08 million The stock is up because they expect to add 1 million subscribers in Q3 but curious if any growth from US/EMEA

again, Netflix: 1. already limits simultaneous streams per account, charging you extra if you want more. 2. just imposed a major price hike. 3. spent years saying password sharing was no big deal https://twitter.com/...

Netflix growth is definitely over. They lost 970K subscribers in Q2 but since they forecasted that it would be 2M, the market response is actually positive. Definitely looks like raising prices was a killer for subscriber growth but they're doubling down. https://www.cnbc.com/...
To underline it: Netflix is losing customers in the market it's been in the longest and where it has the most competition. https://twitter.com/...

Damn. You know your multi-billion media company is clamping down on expenses when instead of paying for Nielsen, they just pull critical industry data from a secondary source https://s22.q4cdn.com/... https://twitter.com/...
Netflix lost 1.3M customers in the US and Canada, and yet its shares are up 8% or so in post-market trading. It's back above $200/share for the first time in more than a month.

no surprise here...netflix loses subs some estimated it was going to be 4 million that wasn't the case, they were able to survive cuz of stranger things and that was a strategic move https://www.cnbc.com/...

Let's bring these bipartisan bills to a vote. #ReinInBigTech https://www.wsj.com/...

in other news today, this is pretty brutal evidence from House Judiciary discovery of Facebook. It sure must be useful for the private, state AGs and FTC lawsuits - the latter seeking to break up the company. https://judiciary.house.gov/ ...

I'm on the Senate floor highlighting the need to pass my bipartisan legislation with @ChuckGrassley to rein in Big Tech. Our bill will help small businesses and consumers by protecting competition on digital marketplaces. https://twitter.com/...

“The advocates' midsummer push is driven in part by a recognition that tech companies have a structural advantage in the legislative fight: If Congress does nothing, the companies win.” https://www.wsj.com/...
Recalling Mark Zuckerberg's assertion: “I'd rather buy than compete.” “To me, that was Exhibit A.”

The internal FB research doc I reported on in 2019 was just published by Congress. IMO, it's a fascinating look at how FB studies network effects and how different social networks (including its own) can/can't coexist https://judiciary.house.gov/ ... https://twitter.com/...

I'm also fascinated by all the strategies other than *improving the product* that are discussed in the memo. They are really leaning into the network effects and making sure they protect their incumbency advantage. This is what “markets that tend towards tipping” look like. 4/

also released today as part of the committee finalizing the 2020 online platform antitrust report was the text of Facebooks Cunningham memo, detailing how the company views competition between its own services as more of a threat than outside companies. https://www.bloomberg.com/...
The @FTC & @Facebook finally agree on something: @Facebook doesn't face any direct competition. https://twitter.com/...

NEW: Meta insists that its social-networking and messaging services have dozens of outside rivals, but an internal memo depicts top executives of Facebook's parent company as more worried about the threat posed by its own products https://www.bloomberg.com/...

NEW: 🇷🇺Russian FSB-backed group #Turla distributed app to people that wanted to help #Ukraine do cyberwar. It was actually android malware. Clever idea, but it doesn't sound like many users fell for it. @Google's blog by @billyleonard https://blog.google/... https://twitter.com/...

Amateurs should not be trying to crowdsource DDoS attacks using dodgy mobile apps. This isn't time for “SETI At Home meets The Matrix.” For the love of God, if you don't even know what a TCP/IP socket is, do not just download “hacker” tools. You're not Neo. https://twitter.com/...

The CyberAzov activity is interesting but I believe there are some flaws in the reporting here. 1. One of the apps at least attempts to continue an attack, so the assertion of single GET requests doesn't appear to be correct (might change purpose hypothesis) 1/n https://twitter.com/...
Surprise! An app that was advertised as a tool to help Ukrainians launch DDoS attacks against Russian sites was, in fact, malware designed to track and identify them. https://twitter.com/...
Interesting find — makes perfect sense to hide an exploit behind this type of appeal. Never forget: social engineering is an essential element of almost every exploit. https://twitter.com/...
This was a strange one. It really feels we are finding more and more creative and strange campaigns from APT actors recently. Keeps things interesting. https://twitter.com/...
